# Any Physical Theory Is Always Provisional

> Any physical theory is always provisional, in the sense that it is only a hypothesis: you can never prove it.

**Citation:** Hawking, Stephen. *A Brief History of Time*. Bantam Books, 1988. Kindle edition. 1   Our Picture of the Universe, p. 3, loc. 87.
